title: selaginella articulata
intro: Selaginella articulata, also known as Indian joint pine or jointed spikemoss, is a type of plant that belongs to the genus Selaginella, which is a group of small, fern-like plants. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. Selaginella articulata is often prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to make various formulations, including capsules and ointments.
